name: Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Slack end to end

parameters.text: =✅ Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Slack end to end — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09221-order`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Map to Canonical Schema

### Map to Canonical Schema

Shapes the item: writes `orderId`, `kind`, `payload`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Route by Event Kind

### Route by Event Kind

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: created, updated + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Handle 'created' → Google Sheets · ②→ Handle 'updated' → Forward API · ③→ Log Unrecognized Kind

### Handle 'created' → Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Handle 'updated' → Forward API

POST https://api.example.com/v1/orders — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Log Unrecognized Kind

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Consolidate Handling

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Respond with Outcome

### Respond with Outcome

Sends the HTTP 200 response back to the caller — keep this fast; heavy work happens after/async.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Slack end to end

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Slack end to end. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual order ops is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Map to Canonical Schema → Route by Event Kind → Handle 'created' → Google Sheets → Consolidate Handling → Respond with Outcome → Save Execution Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating order ops in e-commerce
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Scales without extra headcount
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your order ops process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
